description: Boxcar endwall force limits by nominal capacity (50, 70, 100, 100 ton with 286k lb).
limits_or_values: 50 ton → 100,000 lb total, 35-45% top half, 65-55% bottom half; 70 ton → 220,000 lb; 100 ton (263k lb) → 260,000 lb; 100 ton (286k lb) → 284,000 lb

description: Load weight must not exceed the stenciled load limit or UMLER value; use the lower of the two.
Load on one truck must not exceed half of the load limit.
Secure lading to prevent tipping/moving when vacant crosswise space exceeds 18 inches.
Combined CG of car and load must not exceed 98 inches above top of rail.
Cars exceeding Plate C dimensions may extend to 17 ft above rail. Shippers must calculate combined CG when any part of load exceeds 11 ft 8 in (140 in) above car floor.
limits_or_values: load height > 140 in → CG calculation required

description: Formula to calculate combined CG of car and load.
Mechanical trucks (forklifts) must not be used to open or close freight car doors.
Maintain at least three points of contact between rolls and other rolls or car walls.
Do not load a single centered roll against endwalls for rolls with diameter < half car width (≤ 56 in).
limits_or_values: roll diameter ≤ 56 in → no single centered roll at endwall

description: Do not load consecutive rolls centered in-line unless approved pattern.
Use risers to break the strata line for multiple layer loads.
Center rolls in doorway, equal space each side, no roll-to-door contact.
For inset doorway method, rolls adjacent to doorposts must be at least 50% behind the doorposts.
When stacking rolls of differing widths, place smaller-width rolls below wider rolls.
For 50 and 58 in diameter rolls, if missing rolls are only out of doorway or doorpost area, layers may be stepped down at the end of the boxcar instead of in doorway area.
Select pattern that allows greatest number of floor spots based on car size and roll diameter, without exceeding weight and CG limits.
Limit thickness of sidewall or endwall void filler to the least amount needed, generally ≤ 2 inches.
Steel straps for doorway protection must have minimum breaking strength 4,725 lb and 75% joint efficiency.
Nonmetallic straps for doorway protection must have minimum breaking strength 3,285 lb and 75% joint efficiency.
Lumber is not an approved method of doorway protection for roll paper shipments.

Airbag performance levels: Level 1 for lateral voids; Level 2 for lengthwise voids up to 75k lb; Level 3 up to 160k lb; Level 4 up to 216k lb (and horizontal apps up to 190k lb); Level 5 up to 216k lb (horizontal apps).
limits_or_values: Level 2: ≤ 75,000 lb; Level 3: ≤ 160,000 lb; Level 4: ≤ 216,000 lb (horizontal ≤ 190,000 lb); Level 5: ≤ 216,000 lb

description: Rubber mats between layers must cover ≥ 50% of surface area and be ≥ 2 mm thick.
Rubber mats used as doorway protection must cover floor under doorway rolls with outer edge folded under ≥ 2 inches. Do not overlap mats.
Do not reuse rubber mats when applied as a method of doorway protection.
Lengthwise void fillers must have minimum crush strength of 2,250 psf.
Maximum three fillers per lengthwise void location; maximum filled space 12 inches.
Honeycomb sidewall spacers for 1-1 offset: width ≥ (½ roll diameter + 6 in), height ≥ ½ roll height, crush strength ≥ 2,250 psf.
limits_or_values: width ≥ (roll diameter/2 + 6 in), height ≥ ½ roll height, crush ≥ 2,250 psf

description: Wall liners must have minimum crush strength of 2,250 psf.
Thickness of wall liners generally not more than 2 inches.
Wall liners for 50 in diameter rolls must have minimum width 16 inches.
Minimum height of wall liners must be at least 50% of adjacent roll height.
Minimum riser sizes per Table 5.2 (40 to 72 in diameter).
Minimum combined crush strength for risers: 6,000 psf under certain conditions; 9,000 psf for higher weight, doorway, incomplete layer blocking, or T‑loading.
limits_or_values: 6,000 psf (≤5,000 lb per stack, not incomplete layer blocking, not T‑loading, not doorway); 9,000 psf otherwise

description: All strip risers must have minimum crush strength 9,000 psf.
Risers to block incomplete layers: minimum 6 in tall, maximum 30 in tall.
Risers used in doorway to block incomplete layer: max height 6 in, placed only under single layer rolls.
Up to five pad risers may be laminated together, maximum combined height 30 inches.
Risers to raise doorway rolls for dock plate: single riser, max 4 in for multi‑layer loads; single‑layer loads max 6 in.
Risers to break strata line: height not less than 4 in, not more than 8 in, not used in doorway.
limits_or_values: 4 in ≤ height ≤ 8 in, not in doorway

description: Vertical airbags fill voids from 4 to 12 inches (after inflation).
Airbags must be at least 2/3 the height of adjacent stacks before inflation.
For roll diameter ≤45 in, airbag width ≥ (roll diameter – 10 in).
For roll diameter >45 in, airbag width ≥ (roll diameter – 12 in).
Do not use filler panels adjacent to vertical airbags. If fillers are needed to reduce void space, place them between rolls away from airbag locations.
Do not use airbags in tandem (back‑to‑back in same void or to fill multiple lengthwise voids in the same row).
Level 4 and 5 airbags may be used horizontally; doorway rolls aligned with car centerline.
Horizontal airbags: minimum width 36 inches, height ≥ 2/3 of adjacent stacks.
Horizontal airbags must be at least 6 inches greater than twice the roll diameter in height (or width).
Use one airbag per layer unless specified otherwise in the load plan. When using fewer bags, protect pinch points with buffer material.
Do not use filler panels adjacent to horizontal airbags. If fillers are needed, place them between rolls away from airbag locations.
If doorway single layer rolls >72 in wide, use two same‑size horizontal airbags.
When incomplete layers are loaded at end walls, blocking rolls should be at least two stacks deep from the endwalls.
If roll width ≤ diameter, block incomplete layer by 25% of roll width.
If roll width > diameter, block incomplete layer by 50% of roll width.

58 in pulboard rolls: may be blocked by 6 in blocking under conditions – endwall incomplete layer (≥3 rolls, ≤6 rolls) or middle incomplete layer (≤4 rolls single layer each end); doorway rolls not used as blocking; risers 40×40 in square or strip with 9,000 psf; rubber between layers if second layer complete through doorway.
limits_or_values: blocking height = 6 in, riser crush ≥9,000 psf

description: 50 in rolls in 50 ft cushion boxcars (max 12 ft door) may be blocked at endwall or second stack with 50% blocking using risers; risers laminated up to 30 in, crush strength 6,000 psf.
limits_or_values: blocking = 50% of roll height, riser height ≤30 in, crush ≥6,000 psf

description: Anchored straps must be Type 1A Grade 5 nonmetallic or 1¼×0.029 in steel.
Use one anchored strap for rolls ≤40 in width.
Use two anchored straps for rolls >40 in width, placed at center and ¼ height from top.
Blocking rolls must contact incomplete layers by at least 6 inches.
Anchored straps without blocking rolls: number depends on width/diameter ratio.
formula_or_algorithm: width ≤ diameter → 1 strap per 4 stacks; 1.0<width/diam≤1.5 → 2 straps per 3 stacks; 1.5<width/diam≤2 → 1 strap per 2 stacks; width/diam>2 → 2 straps per 2 stacks

description: Unitized straps must be Type 1A Grade 5 nonmetallic or 1¼×0.029 in steel.
Encompass all rolls in incomplete layer with unitizing straps; use strap holders.
Use one unitizing strap for rolls ≤40 in width.
Use two unitizing straps for rolls >40 in width, placed at center and ¼ height from top.
Blocking rolls must contact incomplete layers by at least 6 inches.
Roll omission allowed for 40/42 in and 58 in diameter rolls.
40/42 in single‑layer loads: load in 2‑1‑2 pattern, omit centered rolls as needed.
40/42 in two‑layer loads: omit centered rolls in second layer; floor layer roll placed on risers.
58 in rolls in 50/60 ft boxcars: load single layer in 1‑1 offset pattern, omit rolls per figures.
limits_or_values: 50 ft: 19‑roll (1 omitted) or 18‑roll (2 omitted); 60 ft: 23‑roll or 22‑roll

description: Before loading 58 in omission pattern, place 6×35 in void filler against opposite sidewalls at each end, secured.
Loading sequence for 58 in omission: roll #1 against filler, roll #2 offset, omit next position, roll #3 next to #2.
Load roll #4 on same sidewall as #1, add another 6×35 in void filler between #4 and sidewall.
Load doorway rolls and secure with any AAR‑approved method; apply doorway protection as required.
Only load consecutive centered in‑line rolls following an approved pattern.
50 in rolls in 50 ft cushion single‑plug boxcars: single layer throughout.
One end: load against three 2½ in filler panels (2,250 psf), centered; add one filler panel between rolls and sidewalls.
Place two additional filler panels between rolls in stacks 4 and 5 and sidewall.
Install three 2½ in filler panels between rolls in stacks 4 and 5, secured.
Maintain good lengthwise row alignment, especially in doorway and centered in‑line rolls.
Use minimum two 48×106 in airbags of appropriate level for load weight.
Doorway rolls centered in car, no roll‑to‑door contact; doorpost rolls loaded 50% behind doorpost.
54 in rolls in 50 ft cushion single‑plug boxcars: single layer throughout.
Vertical airbags applied per 6.1.
Steel or nonmetallic doorway straps required.
54 in rolls in 50 ft standard boxcars with 10 ft door: doorway rolls single layer.
Position rubber mats (≥2 mm) under both doorway rolls, no overlap.
One vertical airbag, pressure 6‑8 psi.
Only pulpboard rolls are recommended for on‑side (bilge or T) loading. Other paper grades are not recommended.
Use only cushion‑equipped boxcars for on‑side (T) loading.
Two layers: first layer rolls on end (approved pattern), second layer rolls on side at ends.
Use appropriate height chocks under each on‑side roll.
58 in pulboard T‑loading with 50% blocking: first layer 1‑1 offset, use sidewall/endwall spacers.
Second‑layer on‑side rolls against endwall, same sidewall as blocking roll; 50 ft: 3 per end, 60 ft: 4 per end.
limits_or_values: 50 ft → 3 on‑side per end; 60 ft → 4 per end

description: Apply two chocks per on‑side roll or one chock block (40 in × 6.25 in × 12 in).
In 60 ft double‑door cars, on‑side roll directly behind blocking roll must have height/width ≥1.5× blocking roll diameter.
Blocking roll must block on‑side roll by at least ½ of on‑side roll diameter.
limits_or_values: blocking height ≥ ½ on‑side roll diameter

description: T‑loaded blocking rolls may use laminated square pad risers, up to five risers, combined height ≤30 in.
When blocking rolls are two rolls stacked, next stack must also block at least 50% of the two‑roll stack.
In 50 ft boxcar, complete load with airbags or 2/3‑height filler panels; apply doorway protection.
In 60 ft double‑door boxcar, complete load with 2/3‑height filler panels; apply doorway protection.
58 in pulboard T‑loading with 14 in chocks: approved for 50/60 ft cars with 3 on‑side rolls per end.
limits_or_values: chock size 14×14×8.5 in, triple‑wall, puncture strength 1,100 lb/sheet

description: Chocks: 14 in long at bottom, 14 in high at back, 8.5 in wide, contoured face, double‑faced tape.
For anchored loads up to 180,000 lb, use six Type 1A Grade 5 straps.
For anchored loads up to 205,000 lb, use seven Type 1A Grade 5 straps.

When lading could fall or roll out of doorway or contact doors, openings must be protected with steel straps or other material of sufficient strength. Cars loaded with cylindrical items (rolls of paper, drums) require doorway protection unless specifically exempted.
Steel straps: one strap per layer for rolls <25 in width.
Steel straps: two straps per layer for rolls ≥25 in width.
Nonmetallic strap substitution for steel limited to door openings ≤12 ft.
Substitution rates: Grade 4 belt‑loop → 3:1; Grade 4 conventional → 4:1; Grades 5,6,7 belt‑loop → 2:1.
Key lock strapping: one strap per layer for rolls <36 in width.
Key lock strapping: two straps per layer for rolls ≥36 in width or single‑layer doorway.
limits_or_values: roll width ≥36 in or single layer → 2 straps/layer

description: Encircling strapping: one unitizing strap per layer (two if single layer or roll width/height >36 in).
limits_or_values: single layer or width>36 in → 2 straps/layer

description: X‑banding: two straps per layer total (four if single layer or roll width/height >36 in).
limits_or_values: single layer or width>36 in → 4 straps/layer

description: Figure‑8 banding: one strap per layer (two if single layer or roll width/height >36 in).
limits_or_values: single layer or width>36 in → 2 straps/layer

description: Anchored U‑straps for 70 in rolls in 60 ft cushioned car: nine 1¼×0.029 in steel straps.
Anchored U‑straps for 72/84 in rolls: three straps per layer for rolls ≤40 in width; four per layer for >40 in; minimum nine total.
limits_or_values: width ≤40 in → 3 straps/layer; width >40 in → 4 straps/layer; total ≥9 straps

description: Anchored S‑straps for 68‑69 in rolls: nine 1¼×0.029 in steel straps.
Anchored S‑straps for 84 in rolls: three per layer (≤40 in wide) or four per layer (>40 in wide); minimum nine total.
Anchored H‑straps for 81 in rolls: four Type 1A Grade 5 nonmetallic straps per layer.
Rubber mats under doorway rolls: minimum 2 mm thick, cover floor space, do not overlap.
Fold outer edge of rubber mat under at least 2 inches.
When void filler used with rubber mats: use 2/3‑height to full‑height void fillers, crush strength ≥2,250 psf, max three panels, max void 12 in.
Rubber mats between layers: minimum 2 mm thick, do not overlap, apply between all layers in doorway.
For rolls 42‑51 in diameter, rubber mats between layers must cover ≥50% of surface area.
For 58 in rolls, rubber mats between layers: 48×52 in; also apply between rolls and floor.

Rubber mats with risers: allowed only with rigid lengthwise fillers, no airbags; risers must be pad risers with 9,000 psf crush strength; mat ≥2 mm thick, same size or larger than riser, placed under and over riser.
Exception: 40 or 42 in rolls loaded triple‑across in‑line in cushion‑equipped cars with single plug doors – no doorway protection required.
Exception: 58 in pulpboard rolls in cushion‑equipped cars with 10‑16 ft plug doors, loaded 1‑1 offset, secured with 2/3‑height void fillers – no doorway protection required.
37 in diameter Kraft rolls, single layer, 60 ft cushioned boxcar, 16 ft plug door: rubber mat floor application, use 2/3 height filler panels.
42 in diameter roll paper, multi‑layer, 60 ft cushioned boxcar, 16 ft plug/sliding door: rubber mats between layers, vertical airbag, banding required if sliding door.
45 in diameter roll paper, multi‑layer, 50/60 ft cushioned boxcar, 16 ft plug door: rubber mats between layers, unitizing strapping required.
48 in diameter roll paper, multi‑layer, 60 ft cushioned boxcar, 16 ft plug/sliding door: rubber mats between layers, unitizing strapping required.
49 in diameter roll paper, multi‑layer, 50/60 ft cushioned boxcar, 16 ft plug door: rubber mats between layers, unitizing strapping required.
50 in diameter roll paper, multi‑layer, 60 ft cushioned boxcar, 16 ft plug/sliding door: rubber mats between layers, unitizing strapping required.
51 in diameter roll paper, multi‑layer, 60 ft cushioned boxcar, 16 ft plug/sliding door: rubber mats between layers, unitizing strapping required.
54 in diameter roll paper, single layer, 50 ft standard/cushioned boxcar, 10 ft plug/sliding door: rubber mat floor application, two centered in‑line rolls, one vertical airbag.
54 in diameter roll paper, single layer, 60 ft cushioned boxcar, 16 ft plug door: rubber mat floor with roll under, use 2/3 height filler panels.
58 in diameter roll paper, single layer, 50/60 ft standard/cushioned boxcar, 16 ft plug/sliding door: rubber mat floor with roll under, see load pattern for airbag requirements.
58 in diameter roll paper, multi‑layer, 50 ft cushioned boxcar, 12 ft plug/sliding door: rubber mats floor and between layers, use 2/3 height filler panels.
60 in diameter cellulose (plastic wrapped), multi‑layer, 60 ft cushioned boxcar, 16 ft plug door: rubber mat floor with roll under, use 2/3 height filler panels.
60 in diameter paperboard, multi‑layer, 60 ft cushioned boxcar, 12 ft plug door: rubber mats between layers only (no floor mat).
60 in diameter roll paper, single layer, 60 ft cushioned boxcar, 16 ft plug door: rubber mat floor with roll under, use 2/3 height filler panels.
68 in diameter roll paper, multi‑layer, 60 ft cushioned boxcar, 12 ft plug door: rubber mats floor and between layers, anchored straps required.
69 in diameter roll paper, multi‑layer, 60 ft cushioned boxcar, 12 ft plug door: rubber mats floor and between layers, anchored straps required.
70 in diameter roll paper, multi‑layer, 60 ft cushioned boxcar, 12 ft plug door: rubber mats floor and between layers, anchored straps required.
